description: Python-to-JavaScript module mapping for skill-translator. Used by the Dry-Run-Pass to classify imports as mappable, partial (with caveats), or unmappable. The translator emits a user-facing modal when partial or unmappable imports are present.

viaValues.npm: Use an npm package via the sandbox esbuild bundler (script imports it as ES module).
viaValues.built-in-tool: Operation should NOT run in the sandbox. The translator routes the call to a Vault Operator built-in tool (create_pptx, create_docx, etc.) instead of translating the Python code.
viaValues.partial: Partial mapping exists with documented limitations.
viaValues.unmappable: No reasonable JavaScript equivalent; user must either accept partial translation or fall back to skill-creator.

json.notes: Direct mapping. json.loads -> JSON.parse, json.dumps -> JSON.stringify.

os.notes: os.path.join -> path.join. File system operations route through ctx.vault sandbox bridge.
limitations: No process management (os.system, os.exec)
No env-var write (os.environ assignment)

sys.notes: sys.argv -> use execute(args) signature. sys.stdout.write -> return value or console.debug.
limitations: No sys.exit (use return)
No sys.path manipulation

re.notes: re.search -> str.match, re.sub -> str.replace. Watch for Python-specific regex features (named groups syntax differs).

datetime.notes: datetime.now() -> new Date(). Use ISO strings for serialization. For complex parsing prefer date-fns via npm.

pathlib.notes: Path('foo')/'bar' -> path.join('foo','bar'). All file ops route through ctx.vault.

io.notes: io.StringIO -> string concatenation. io.BytesIO -> Uint8Array. File reads via ctx.vault.read.
limitations: No seek()/tell()-style stream cursor

csv.notes: csv.reader -> Papa.parse. csv.writer -> Papa.unparse. Handles delimiters + quoting.

yaml.notes: yaml.safe_load -> yaml.load with FAILSAFE_SCHEMA. yaml.dump -> yaml.dump.

requests.notes: requests.get -> ctx.requestUrl({url, method:'GET'}). Replaces all HTTP via the sandbox bridge (CORS-safe).
limitations: No streaming download
No multipart upload (use ctx.vault.writeBinary then ctx.requestUrl)

urllib.notes: urllib.request.urlopen -> ctx.requestUrl. Same constraints as requests.

pdfplumber.notes: PDF text extraction should NOT happen in the sandbox. The translator emits a Body-instruction that uses read_document for input and the appropriate built-in tool for output.

PyPDF2.notes: Same as pdfplumber. Avoid translating binary PDF manipulation; reroute to built-in tools.

python-pptx.notes: PPTX generation routes through the built-in create_pptx tool (template-mode) or plan_presentation (content planning). DO NOT translate Presentation/Slide manipulation.

python-docx.notes: DOCX generation routes through the built-in create_docx tool. DO NOT translate Document/Paragraph manipulation.

openpyxl.notes: XLSX generation routes through the built-in create_xlsx tool. DO NOT translate Workbook/Worksheet manipulation.

pandas.notes: Common pandas patterns (DataFrame.head, .groupby, .to_csv) map to arrays + danfojs. Complex statistical operations may need manual coding.
limitations: No HDF5
No SQL backend
Performance lower than native pandas

numpy.notes: Element-wise math maps to Array.map. Matrix ops need ndarray or manual nested-loop translation. For scipy-grade math, fall back to skill-creator.
limitations: No FFT/signal processing
No advanced linear algebra

scipy.notes: No comparable scientific-computing library exists in JavaScript. The translator stops the conversion when scipy is imported.
limitations: Hard stop: signals, statistics, optimization, integration

matplotlib.notes: Plot generation should produce data + use generate_canvas or external charting; matplotlib code itself does not translate.
limitations: Hard stop on pyplot.show / savefig

sklearn.notes: ML model inference may be possible via ONNX, but training pipelines do not translate.
limitations: Hard stop: model.fit, GridSearchCV

subprocess.notes: Process spawning is not available in the sandbox by design (security).
limitations: Hard stop

shutil.notes: shutil.copyfile -> read + write through ctx.vault. shutil.rmtree -> ctx.vault.list + per-file remove (not exposed; needs plugin op).
limitations: No archive helpers (use built-in tools)

tempfile.notes: tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile -> write into the plugin tmp folder. Cleanup is automatic on task end.

hashlib.notes: hashlib.sha256(b).hexdigest() -> crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-256', buf) + hex. Async API in browser.

collections.notes: Counter -> manual reduce + Map. defaultdict -> use Map with || init. OrderedDict -> Map preserves insertion order.

itertools.notes: chain -> spread/concat. groupby -> manual reduce. permutations -> needs manual algorithm or a small helper.
limitations: No lazy-evaluation generators by default; manual implementation needed

functools.notes: lru_cache -> manual Map memoization. partial -> arrow function bind.

typing.notes: Type hints are dropped in translation; TypeScript types may be added in a follow-up if the target is .ts.

logging.notes: logger.info -> console.debug. logger.warning -> console.warn. logger.error -> console.error. No file handler in sandbox.

argparse.notes: Argparse defines a CLI shape; in Vault Operator scripts the `execute(args)` parameter object replaces it. The translator inlines a destructuring pattern at the top of execute().

click.notes: Same as argparse: the click CLI shape collapses into the execute(args) destructuring pattern. Subcommands map to a top-level `if (args.command === ...)` switch.

typer.notes: Same handling as click/argparse: typed CLI definition becomes the args object destructured in execute().

dataclasses.notes: @dataclass classes become plain JS objects. Field defaults map to a factory function. For TypeScript targets, emit an `interface` declaration alongside the factory.

enum.notes: `class Color(Enum): RED = 1` -> `const Color = Object.freeze({ RED: 1, BLUE: 2 });`. For TS targets prefer `as const` literal types.

math.notes: math.sqrt -> Math.sqrt. math.pi -> Math.PI. math.inf -> Infinity. math.isnan -> Number.isNaN.

random.notes: random.random -> Math.random. random.randint(a,b) -> Math.floor(Math.random()*(b-a+1))+a. random.choice -> arr[Math.floor(Math.random()*arr.length)]. For crypto-grade randomness use crypto.getRandomValues.

time.notes: time.time() -> Date.now()/1000. time.sleep(s) -> await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, s*1000)). time.monotonic() -> performance.now()/1000.
limitations: No synchronous sleep -- the sandbox is single-threaded; convert callers to async

string.notes: string.ascii_letters -> 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ'. string.digits -> '0123456789'. string.Template -> template literals or a tiny custom function.

pprint.notes: pprint(obj) -> console.debug(JSON.stringify(obj, null, 2)). Skip if used purely for debugging; production code rarely needs it.

importlib.notes: importlib.import_module('foo.bar') with a CONSTANT name -> rewrite as a top-level `import * as bar from './foo/bar.js'` plus a `MODULES = { bar }` dispatch object. importlib with a runtime-computed name (plugin discovery, dynamic registries) is unmappable because the sandbox blocks dynamic `import()`.
limitations: Runtime-computed module names cannot be resolved at bundle time
No importlib.reload (modules are immutable in the sandbox)

pkgutil.notes: Package introspection (iter_modules, walk_packages) requires a runtime module graph that the sandbox does not expose.

glob.notes: glob.glob('Inbox/*.md') -> (await ctx.vault.list('Inbox')).filter(p => /\.md$/.test(p)). For recursive ** use a custom walker over ctx.vault.list.
limitations: No glob in hidden folders unless ctx.vault path explicitly targets them

fnmatch.notes: fnmatch.fnmatch(name, pattern) -> compile the glob pattern to a RegExp (* -> .*, ? -> ., [seq] -> [seq]). For complex cases use the `minimatch` npm package.

configparser.notes: ConfigParser().read(...) -> ini.parse(text). Section.set -> mutate the parsed object then ini.stringify. Use `ini` from npm.

tomllib.notes: tomllib.loads (Python 3.11+) -> smol-toml's parse. tomllib.dumps -> smol-toml's stringify.

toml.notes: Same as tomllib. The `toml` PyPI package and the stdlib `tomllib` both map to smol-toml.

xml.notes: xml.etree.ElementTree.parse -> new XMLParser().parse(text). For round-trip XML emission use XMLBuilder.
limitations: Namespace handling and XPath features beyond simple element access need careful translation

lxml.notes: lxml.etree -> fast-xml-parser. lxml.html -> cheerio (jquery-like DOM traversal). XPath queries beyond simple paths need manual conversion.

html.notes: html.parser.HTMLParser -> cheerio.load(html). html.escape -> simple replace chain or use the `entities` npm package.

beautifulsoup4.notes: Same as bs4 -- the PyPI distribution name.

markdown.notes: markdown.markdown(text) -> marked.parse(text). Plugins/extensions map onto marked extensions or markdown-it when complex AST handling is needed.

markdown_it.notes: markdown-it has both Python and JS implementations; the API is essentially the same.

pydantic.notes: BaseModel subclasses -> z.object({ ... }) schemas. .model_validate -> schema.parse. .model_dump -> just return the object. For advanced features (validators, root_validator) translate to zod refinements.

jsonschema.notes: jsonschema.validate(instance, schema) -> new Ajv().compile(schema)(instance). Error reporting shape differs slightly.

asyncio.notes: async def -> async function. await -> await. asyncio.gather(*coros) -> Promise.all([...]). asyncio.run(main()) -> just `await main()` because execute() is already async. asyncio.sleep -> new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, ms)).
limitations: No event-loop primitives (loop.run_until_complete, loop.add_signal_handler)
No asyncio.Queue (use a simple array + Promise)

concurrent.notes: concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor.map -> Promise.all(items.map(fn)). ProcessPoolExecutor is unmappable -- the sandbox has no worker pool.
limitations: Only IO concurrency; no CPU-parallel workers

threading.notes: Real OS threads are not exposed in the sandbox. Convert thread-based patterns to async/await with Promise.all.
limitations: Hard stop: Thread, Lock, RLock, Semaphore beyond simple Promise-chained patterns

multiprocessing.notes: No Process equivalent in the sandbox. Patterns relying on process isolation cannot be translated.

queue.notes: queue.Queue -> use a plain array with shift/push when single-consumer; for producer/consumer use a simple Promise-based async iterator.
limitations: No thread-safe blocking semantics; the sandbox is single-threaded

aiohttp.notes: aiohttp.ClientSession().get(url) -> ctx.requestUrl({url, method:'GET'}). Server-side aiohttp.web is unmappable.
limitations: No long-lived sessions, no streaming responses

httpx.notes: httpx.get / httpx.AsyncClient.get -> ctx.requestUrl. Same constraints as requests/aiohttp.
limitations: No HTTP/2 streaming, no connection pooling

flask.notes: Web servers do not run inside the sandbox. Skills that expose HTTP endpoints belong in a separate Plugin/MCP-server, not in the sandbox-script layer.
limitations: Hard stop: Flask app, route decorators, request/response objects

fastapi.notes: Same as flask. FastAPI's dependency-injection and pydantic-based request schemas are server-side patterns that cannot run in the sandbox.

starlette.notes: Same as fastapi. ASGI servers are not a sandbox concept.

uvicorn.notes: ASGI server. Not a sandbox concept.

socket.notes: Raw sockets are not exposed in the sandbox by design (only ctx.requestUrl over HTTPS is available).

ssl.notes: TLS is handled by ctx.requestUrl; the user-facing certificate API is not exposed.

dotenv.notes: process.env is not visible to the sandbox. Pass secrets explicitly via args or store them via update_settings; do not load .env files at runtime.

PIL.notes: Image manipulation requires binary canvas operations that the sandbox does not expose. Route image generation through built-in tools (create_drawio for diagrams, create_pptx for slide images) or skill-creator for a custom plugin tool.

Pillow.notes: Same as PIL (the PyPI distribution name).

cv2.notes: Computer vision (OpenCV) is not portable to the sandbox.

scikit_learn.notes: Alias for sklearn -- ML inference may be possible via ONNX, training is unmappable.

torch.notes: PyTorch is unmappable. Pre-trained models can be exported to ONNX and called via the plugin's onnxruntime-web, but training and pytorch-specific APIs do not translate.

tensorflow.notes: Same as torch -- runtime via ONNX export is possible, but tf APIs do not translate.

transformers.notes: Hugging Face transformers requires PyTorch/TensorFlow. Use ONNX exports + onnxruntime-web for inference, or route to ctx.requestUrl-based hosted inference (OpenRouter, Replicate, HF Inference Endpoints).

openai.notes: Translate openai.chat.completions.create(...) into ctx.requestUrl POST against https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions with the appropriate Authorization header. The user must supply the API key via args.
limitations: No native client SDK; streaming responses need manual chunk parsing

anthropic.notes: Translate anthropic.messages.create(...) into ctx.requestUrl POST against https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages with x-api-key + anthropic-version headers.
limitations: No native client SDK; tool-use streaming needs manual chunk parsing

google.notes: google.generativeai -> ctx.requestUrl against https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com. google-cloud-* SDKs (auth, storage, etc.) are unmappable inside the sandbox.
limitations: No google-cloud-* SDKs (auth helpers and per-service clients unavailable)
No native streaming generate-content responses

boto3.notes: AWS SDK relies on signed-request helpers + credential providers that the sandbox cannot host. Use a separate Plugin/MCP-server.

rich.notes: Rich console output is not rendered in the sandbox. Strip styles and emit plain console.debug for diagnostics.

tqdm.notes: tqdm wraps iterables for progress bars. In Vault Operator scripts emit `console.debug('progress: i/n')` or call ctx.pushProgress when the host context exposes it.
limitations: No terminal control characters; progress is line-based

tabulate.notes: Render Markdown tables manually: header row + separator + cell rows. For grid output use the `cli-table3` npm package.

humanize.notes: Common humanize.naturaltime / humanize.naturalsize can be replicated with a few formatter functions. For full coverage use `humanize-duration` + `pretty-bytes` from npm.
limitations: No locale-data bundle inside the sandbox; English-only by default

pytz.notes: Use Intl.DateTimeFormat with the IANA timezone string (`Europe/Berlin`). For complex DST arithmetic use the `luxon` npm package.

dateutil.notes: dateutil.parser.parse -> date-fns parseISO or luxon DateTime.fromISO. relativedelta -> luxon.Duration.

babel.notes: babel.numbers.format_currency -> Intl.NumberFormat with style:'currency'. i18n message catalogs map to i18next.
limitations: No locale-data files inside the sandbox

grep.notes: Read the target file via ctx.vault and apply a JS regex.

sed.notes: Simple substitutions map to string.replace. Multi-line sed scripts may need manual translation.

awk.notes: Simple field extraction maps to split + map. Complex awk programs (associative arrays, BEGIN/END blocks) need careful translation.
limitations: Multi-pattern programs may need manual rewriting

python.notes: Recursive python invocation is unmappable; the translator focuses on direct Python source files.

pip.notes: Package installation is not part of the runtime; the translator surfaces unmappable on encountering pip calls.
